competencies.competency_text: Observes and identifies the natural (insects, plants, birds, animals, geographical features, sun and moon, stars, planets, natural resources) and social (houses, relationships) components in their immediate environment
competencies.justification: In 'Pictures of the Sky' students observe and record changes in the Sun's colour, position, and brightness across morning, afternoon, and night, and learn the Sun is the brightest sky object giving light and heat. In 'Night Sky' students observe and draw star patterns and the changing shapes of the Moon across different nights.

competencies.competency_text: Describes relationships (including between humans and animals/nature) and traditions (art forms, celebrations, festivals) in the family and community
competencies.justification: In 'The Shadows' students learn about shadow puppetry as a long-practised Indian art form and about the Jantar Mantar/Samrat Yantra as cultural heritage. In 'Night Sky' students describe festivals tied to Moon phases (Diwali on Amavasya, Eid on first crescent, Rakshabandhan/Guru Poornima/Buddha Poornima on Poornima, Pongal, Chhath Puja, Karva Chauth). In 'India's Chandrayaan Mission' students write about festivals in their own family/community related to the Sun or Moon.

competencies.competency_text: Asks questions and makes predictions about simple patterns (season change, food chain, phases of the moon, movement of stars and planets, shapes of trees, plants, leaves, and flowers, rituals, celebrations) observed in the immediate environment
competencies.justification: In 'Pictures of the Sky' students observe and discuss patterns in sky and Sun changes across the day. In 'Night Sky' students discuss whether the Moon's shape changes follow a pattern and observe Moon phases across different nights. In 'India's Chandrayaan Mission' the Enquire task explicitly requires students to formulate at least two questions about the Sun's apparent East-to-West movement — directly practising question-asking about sky-object movement patterns.

competencies.competency_text: Describes the relationship between the natural environment and cultural practices in their immediate environment (nature of work, food, festivals, traditions)
competencies.justification: In 'The Shadows' the chapter connects the Sun's apparent motion (natural) to the invention of sundials and India's Jantar Mantar observatories including the Samrat Yantra — a direct natural-to-cultural-practice relationship. In 'Night Sky' the chapter explicitly maps Moon phases (Amavasya, Poornima, first crescent) to specific festivals (Diwali, Rakshabandhan, Eid, Pongal, Chhath Puja, Karva Chauth), and notes that Moon phases gave rise to the earliest calendars. Students then write about their own community's Sun- or Moon-related festivals.

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: In 'The Shadows' the Find out activity directs students to set up a darkened room with a torch and stick, systematically observe how shadow size changes with object distance from the light source, and how shadow direction changes when the torch direction changes — a structured independent inquiry answering specific causal questions about shadow formation.

competencies.competency_text: Presents observations and findings through different creative modes (drawing, diagram, poem, play, skit, oral and written expression)
competencies.justification: Students present observations through multiple creative modes across all sections: drawing morning/afternoon/night skies and writing in an observation table ('Pictures of the Sky'); drawing Sun positions with corresponding shadows ('The Shadows'); drawing observed Moon shapes and star patterns ('Night Sky'); and writing a sky-inspired poem and completing a riddle ('India's Chandrayaan Mission').
